mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and capital demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day horizons in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, with a mean return below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.0 percent year-over-year threshold, anticipated central bank tightening and margin compression force negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The concurrent signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces a negative or zero rank IC over 20-day horizons across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with a rolling rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals acute supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% month-over-month.
